item.title: Soda challenge except I can’t taste or smell :’) I was convince...
item.description:
Soda challenge except I can’t taste or smell :’) I was convinced I could tell the difference between cola / sprite but no… I just killed a part of my soul instead
FULL POST | EMBED | REVERSED

item.title: Who would have thought a lemon could cause so much damage?? Anyw...
item.description:
Who would have thought a lemon could cause so much damage?? Anyways… this recipe actually slaps and one of the first I followed when I started cooking :) it’s from Priya Krishna!! Marinade ingredients: 8 minced garlic cloves, 3tbsp lemon juice, 2tbsp minced ginger, 3tbsp olive oil, 1tbsp finely chopped cilantro, 1tbsp finely chopped mint, 1tbsp coriander, 1tsp amchur powder, 1tsp turmeric, 3/4tsp salt, 1/2tsp red chili powder Combine marinade with 4 chicken breasts! Let rest in the fridge for 2 hrs. Preheat large pan on med-high heat. Add 1tsp oil and chicken. Cook each side for 2 min. Cover, and reduce heat to low and cook for 10 min. Remove from heat and let sit covered for 10-15 min! Don’t look because it’ll remove steam.chicken should be fully cooked but make sure it’s 165F! I served with a little salad and rice! Enjoy 🥳
FULL POST | EMBED | REVERSED

item.title: new (positive!!) TBI updates !!! hopefully this helps with any ...
item.description:
new (positive!!) TBI updates !!! hopefully this helps with any confusion on my loss of “taste” and smell! And huge win for my eye. 😊 Let me know any questions you have and I can answer it in a long form YouTube video haha. I can talk about this for hours but I think this sums it up pretty well A few months ago my neurologist told me to slowly add activities back into my routine and if I don’t get a headache - I can keep doing them (hiking, walking, running, etc). I don’t have a doctor currently telling me what I can and can’t do, since it’s very subjective. But I’m just trying to listen to my body (and brain lol). I’m really hoping this month is when I can start running again! I appreciate all you amazing people who work in healthcare. especially the nurses and those who cared for me in the ICU!! But I think we can all agree the health care here often sucks. So at this point I’m just waiting to slowly gain some more smells which will help my taste come back. I am going to look into more natural/holistic remedies (acupuncture, stem cell, etc) but currently been focused on taking it day by day. I’ll keep you posted on if I gain any new smells or progress! But other than that, I think I’m in a good spot and grateful it wasn’t worse. Thanks for making space for me to vent and talk about this. All your support has meant the world 🫶🏼
FULL POST | EMBED | REVERSED
